title: Introduction to ggadjustedforest
code: # Install from GitHub (CRAN submission pending) # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("kriz98/gg_adjusted_forest")

title: Introduction to ggadjustedforest
text: building multivariable models causal inference, exposure interest causal estimand applies . coefficients adjusted covariates (confounders) can however misinterpreted presented together estimand interest. Reporting can even mislead readers, confounder coefficients identified causal model, susceptible absorbing collider bias, mediation pathways, artefacts depending causal structures (Hernán & Robins, Causal Inference: , 2020; Westreich & Greenland, J Epidemiol, 2013). STROBE guidelines (Vandenbroucke et al., 2007) recent work estimand framework (ICH E9(R1), 2019) emphasise reporting clearly distinguish target quantity nuisance parameters. ggadjustedforest operationalises principle fits models specify exposes coefficient interest plot table, hiding confounder estimates design. also provide cumulative adjustment option, show effect adjustment model building exploratory phases studies.

title: Introduction to ggadjustedforest
text: returned object three components: render plot:
code: library(ggadjustedforest) #> ggadjustedforest 0.1.0 -- Forest plots for exposure effects, hiding confounders by design. #> See `?gg_adjusted_forest` to get started.  data(mtcars) mtcars$am <- as.integer(mtcars$am)   # binary outcome: automatic (0) vs manual (1)  result <- gg_adjusted_forest(   data       = mtcars,   outcome    = "am",   exposure   = "hp",   covariates = c("wt", "cyl"),   model_type = "logistic",   title      = "Effect of Horsepower on Transmission Type" ) #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred result$table #> # A tibble: 2 × 6 #>   model      estimate conf.low conf.high p.value     n #>   <fct>         <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl>   <dbl> <int> #> 1 Unadjusted    0.992    0.979      1.00  0.181     32 #> 2 Adjusted      1.03     1.00       1.09  0.0840    32 result$plot

title: Introduction to ggadjustedforest
text: time--event outcomes supply model_type = "coxph" along time_var event_var:
code: lung <- survival::lung lung$status01 <- as.integer(lung$status == 2) lung <- stats::na.omit(lung[, c("time", "status01", "age", "sex", "ph.ecog")])  result_cox <- gg_adjusted_forest(   data       = lung,   outcome    = "status01",   exposure   = "age",   covariates = c("sex", "ph.ecog"),   model_type = "coxph",   time_var   = "time",   event_var  = "status01",   title      = "Effect of Age on Survival (lung cancer)" ) result_cox$plot

title: Introduction to ggadjustedforest
text: major aesthetic parameters exposed:  linear models x-axis original scale (log):
code: gg_adjusted_forest(   data           = mtcars,   outcome        = "am",   exposure       = "hp",   covariates     = "wt",   model_type     = "logistic",   color          = "#2166ac",   point_size     = 5,   point_shape    = 18,          # diamond   vline_color    = "firebrick",   vline_linetype = "dotted",   x_breaks       = c(0.9, 1.0, 1.1),   title          = "Custom aesthetics" )$plot #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred gg_adjusted_forest(   data       = mtcars,   outcome    = "mpg",   exposure   = "hp",   covariates = c("wt", "cyl"),   model_type = "linear",   title      = "Effect of Horsepower on Fuel Efficiency" )$plot

title: Introduction to ggadjustedforest
text: ggadjustedforest intentionally provide built-multi-outcome wrapper. outcome deserves carefully specified model, bundling single function call obscures . Instead, fit outcome separately stack plots patchwork, already dependency ggadjustedforest:  approach gives full control panel — different covariate sets, model types, axis scales per outcome. / operator stacks plots vertically; use | side--side. Pass plot_layout(guides = "collect") share legend needed.
code: library(patchwork)  data(mtcars) mtcars$am <- as.integer(mtcars$am) mtcars$vs <- as.integer(mtcars$vs)  # Extract $plot — patchwork composes ggplot2 objects directly p_am <- gg_adjusted_forest(   data = mtcars, outcome = "am", exposure = "hp",   covariates = c("wt", "cyl"), model_type = "logistic",   title = "Transmission", show_table = FALSE )$plot  p_vs <- gg_adjusted_forest(   data = mtcars, outcome = "vs", exposure = "hp",   covariates = c("wt", "cyl"), model_type = "logistic",   title = "Engine Shape", show_table = FALSE )$plot  p_am / p_vs

title: Introduction to ggadjustedforest

Westreich D, Greenland S (2013). table 2 fallacy: presenting interpreting confounder modifier coefficients. J Epidemiol 177(4): 292–298. ICH E9(R1) (2019). Statistical Principles Clinical Trials: Addendum Estimands Sensitivity Analysis Clinical Trials. ICH Harmonised Guideline.

what: Publication Ready Forest Plots for Estimand of Interest
title: Publication Ready Forest Plots for Estimand of Interest
text: ggadjustedforest creates publication-quality forest plots effect-size tables display unadjusted adjusted estimates user-specified exposure variable interest — hiding confounder coefficients design, accordance causal inference principles (“Table 2 fallacy”, Westreich & Greenland 2013).

title: Publication Ready Forest Plots for Estimand of Interest
text: estimand interest effect single exposure variable, reporting confounder coefficients alongside misleading: coefficients identified causal model depend full causal structure. ggadjustedforest makes easy present exposure effect cleanly, adjustment.

title: Publication Ready Forest Plots for Estimand of Interest
code: # Install from GitHub (CRAN submission pending) # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("kriz98/gg_adjusted_forest")

title: Publication Ready Forest Plots for Estimand of Interest
text: examples use colon_s finalfit package — 929 colon cancer patients NCCTG trial. research question whether >4 positive lymph nodes (node4) increases 5-year mortality (mort_5yr), adjusting patient tumour characteristics.
code: library(ggadjustedforest) library(finalfit)  # for colon_s  data(colon_s) colon_s$died_5yr <- as.integer(colon_s$mort_5yr == "Died")  confounders <- c("age", "sex.factor", "extent.factor", "differ.factor", "surg.factor")  # Unadjusted vs fully adjusted — pipe-friendly result <- colon_s |>   gg_adjusted_forest(     outcome    = "died_5yr",     exposure   = "node4",     covariates = confounders,     model_type = "logistic",     title      = "Effect of Lymph Node Involvement on 5-Year Mortality"   ) result$plot result$table          # tibble of numeric estimates result$formatted_table  # tibble with "OR (lower–upper)" strings  # Cumulative adjustment — watch the estimate evolve as confounders are added result_cum <- gg_adjusted_forest(   data       = colon_s,   outcome    = "died_5yr",   exposure   = "node4",   covariates = confounders,   model_type = "logistic",   cumulative = TRUE,   cumulative_labels = c(     "Unadjusted"                                                         = "Unadjusted",     "+ age"                                                              = "+ Age",     "+ age + sex.factor"                                                 = "+ Sex",     "+ age + sex.factor + extent.factor"                                 = "+ Extent of spread",     "+ age + sex.factor + extent.factor + differ.factor"                 = "+ Tumour differentiation",     "+ age + sex.factor + extent.factor + differ.factor + surg.factor"   = "+ Time from surgery"   ),   title = "Cumulative Adjustment: Lymph Node Involvement on 5-Year Mortality" ) result_cum$plot  # Multiple outcomes — stack with patchwork (already a dependency) library(patchwork) p1 <- gg_adjusted_forest(colon_s, "died_5yr", "node4", confounders,                           model_type = "logistic", title = "5-Year Mortality",                           show_table = FALSE)$plot p2 <- gg_adjusted_forest(colon_s, "status",   "node4", confounders,                           model_type = "logistic", title = "Death (all follow-up)",                           show_table = FALSE)$plot p1 / p2  # Cox proportional hazards cox_result <- gg_adjusted_forest(   data       = colon_s,   outcome    = "status",   exposure   = "node4",   covariates = confounders,   model_type = "coxph",   time_var   = "time.years",   event_var  = "status",   title      = "Hazard of Death by Lymph Node Involvement" ) cox_result$plot

title: Publication Ready Forest Plots for Estimand of Interest

what: Extract a formatted effect-size table without generating a plot — forest_table
title: Extract a formatted effect-size table without generating a plot — forest_table
text: convenience wrapper around gg_adjusted_forest() returns formatted table. Useful want numeric summaries without producing graphic.

title: Extract a formatted effect-size table without generating a plot — forest_table
code: forest_table(   data,   outcome,   exposure,   covariates = NULL,   model_type = "logistic",   cumulative = FALSE,   cumulative_labels = NULL,   conf_level = 0.95,   time_var = NULL,   event_var = NULL,   strata = NULL,   cluster = NULL,   weights = NULL,   table_digits = 2 )

title: Extract a formatted effect-size table without generating a plot — forest_table
text: data data frame containing variables. outcome Character string. Name outcome variable (ignored Cox models - use time_var event_var instead). exposure Character string. Name exposure variable interest. covariates Character vector confounder/covariate names. non-cumulative mode covariates added together; cumulative mode added one time order supplied. Default NULL produces unadjusted estimate. model_type Character. One "logistic" (default), "linear", "poisson", "coxph". cumulative Logical. TRUE, fit models progressively add one covariate time show step separate row. Default FALSE. cumulative_labels Optional named character vector rename cumulative model labels. Names match auto-generated labels (e.g., "+ age", "+ age + sex"); values replacement labels. conf_level Numeric. Confidence level intervals. Default 0.95. time_var Character. Name time variable (Cox model ). event_var Character. Name event indicator variable (Cox model ; 0/1 logical). strata Character. Name stratification variable Cox models. Default NULL. cluster Character. Name clustering variable cluster-robust standard errors. Requires sandwich lmtest packages. Default NULL. weights Character. Name survey/frequency weight variable. Default NULL. table_digits Integer. Number decimal places table. Default 2.

title: Extract a formatted effect-size table without generating a plot — forest_table
text: data frame columns: model Row label (e.g., "Unadjusted", "Adjusted"). estimate Point estimate (formatted character). ci Confidence interval character string (e.g., "0.95–1.42"). formatted Combined estimate CI (e.g., "1.15 (0.95–1.42)"). p.value Formatted p-value character string. n Number observations.

title: Extract a formatted effect-size table without generating a plot — forest_table
code: data(mtcars) mtcars$am <- as.integer(mtcars$am) forest_table(   data       = mtcars,   outcome    = "am",   exposure   = "hp",   covariates = c("wt", "cyl"),   model_type = "logistic" ) #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred #> # A tibble: 2 × 6 #>   model      estimate ci        formatted        p.value     n #>   <chr>      <chr>    <chr>     <chr>            <chr>   <int> #> 1 Unadjusted 0.99     0.98–1.00 0.99 (0.98–1.00) 0.181      32 #> 2 Adjusted   1.03     1.00–1.09 1.03 (1.00–1.09) 0.084      32

what: Forest plot of unadjusted and adjusted effects for a single exposure — gg_adjusted_forest
title: Forest plot of unadjusted and adjusted effects for a single exposure — gg_adjusted_forest
text: Creates publication-quality forest plot showing unadjusted adjusted (cumulatively adjusted) effect estimates specified exposure variable, hiding confounder coefficients accordance causal inference principles.

title: Forest plot of unadjusted and adjusted effects for a single exposure — gg_adjusted_forest
code: gg_adjusted_forest(   data,   outcome,   exposure,   covariates = NULL,   model_type = "logistic",   cumulative = FALSE,   cumulative_labels = NULL,   effect_label = NULL,   title = NULL,   ref_line = NULL,   point_size = 4,   point_shape = 15,   line_size = 0.7,   color = "black",   colour = NULL,   vline_color = "grey50",   vline_linetype = "dashed",   x_limits = NULL,   x_breaks = NULL,   log_scale = TRUE,   conf_level = 0.95,   time_var = NULL,   event_var = NULL,   strata = NULL,   cluster = NULL,   weights = NULL,   show_table = TRUE,   table_digits = 2 )

title: Forest plot of unadjusted and adjusted effects for a single exposure — gg_adjusted_forest
text: data data frame containing variables. outcome Character string. Name outcome variable (ignored Cox models - use time_var event_var instead). exposure Character string. Name exposure variable interest. covariates Character vector confounder/covariate names. non-cumulative mode covariates added together; cumulative mode added one time order supplied. Default NULL produces unadjusted estimate. model_type Character. One "logistic" (default), "linear", "poisson", "coxph". cumulative Logical. TRUE, fit models progressively add one covariate time show step separate row. Default FALSE. cumulative_labels Optional named character vector rename cumulative model labels. Names match auto-generated labels (e.g., "+ age", "+ age + sex"); values replacement labels. effect_label Character. X-axis label. Defaults "Odds Ratio (95 \% CI)" logistic, "Risk Ratio (95 \% CI)" Poisson, "Hazard Ratio (95 \% CI)" Cox, "Coefficient (95 \% CI)" linear. title Character. Plot title. Default NULL (title). ref_line Numeric. Position vertical reference line. Defaults 1 ratio models 0 linear. point_size Numeric. Size point estimate symbol. Default 4. point_shape Integer. ggplot2 shape code. Default 15 (filled square). line_size Numeric. Thickness CI lines. Default 0.7. color Character. Colour points CI lines. Default "black". colour Alias color (British English spelling). vline_color Character. Colour reference line. Default "grey50". vline_linetype Character. Linetype reference line. Default "dashed". x_limits Numeric vector length 2. Manual x-axis limits. Default NULL (automatic). x_breaks Numeric vector. Manual x-axis break positions. Default NULL (automatic). log_scale Logical. Use log scale x-axis ratio models. Default TRUE. conf_level Numeric. Confidence level intervals. Default 0.95. time_var Character. Name time variable (Cox model ). event_var Character. Name event indicator variable (Cox model ; 0/1 logical). strata Character. Name stratification variable Cox models. Default NULL. cluster Character. Name clustering variable cluster-robust standard errors. Requires sandwich lmtest packages. Default NULL. weights Character. Name survey/frequency weight variable. Default NULL. show_table Logical. Combine forest plot formatted table panel (using patchwork). Default TRUE. table_digits Integer. Number decimal places table. Default 2.

title: Forest plot of unadjusted and adjusted effects for a single exposure — gg_adjusted_forest
text: object class ggadjustedforest (list) components: plot combined ggplot2/patchwork plot object. show_table = FALSE just forest plot. table data frame columns model, estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value, n. formatted_table data frame formatted column containing strings like "1.23 (1.01-1.55)".

title: Forest plot of unadjusted and adjusted effects for a single exposure — gg_adjusted_forest
code: # Logistic regression example data(mtcars) mtcars$am <- as.integer(mtcars$am)   # binary outcome result <- gg_adjusted_forest(   data        = mtcars,   outcome     = "am",   exposure    = "hp", title: ggadjustedforest 0.1.0
text: gg_adjusted_forest(): main function producing forest plots tables unadjusted adjusted exposure effects. forest_table(): standalone table extraction helper. Supports "logistic", "linear", "poisson", "coxph" model types. Cumulative adjustment mode (cumulative = TRUE). Optional cluster-robust standard errors via sandwich / lmtest. Optional survey weights. patchwork-based combined plot + table display. S3 print plot methods ggadjustedforest objects.
